description: Pre-authentication endpoint mapping of the SFOS Jetty web application via static analysis of Java class files and web.xml configuration

web_architecture.web_server: Apache httpd (front-end proxy, ModSecurity WAF with CRS SQLi rules)
web_architecture.origin: Cyberoam (acquired by Sophos) -- package names: cyberoam.*, com.sophos.sf.*

order: 2. RequestCheckFilter -- /* (all requests) -- blocks pre-auth multipart EXCEPT /webconsole/APIController
3. SessionCheckFilter -- /* (all requests) -- checks session validity, exempts listed URIs

pre_auth_operations.description: These operation modes can be invoked without authentication via the exempted servlet endpoints. The 'mode' parameter in the request selects the operation.

webconsole_pre_auth_modes.description: Unknown -- no opcode set, mode only

webconsole_pre_auth_modes.risk: HIGH -- pre-auth user creation with SQL queries and ProcessExecutor

webconsole_pre_auth_modes.description: Unknown -- present in both webconsole and userportal pre-auth

high_priority_attack_vectors.description: The RequestCheckFilter explicitly allows pre-auth multipart file uploads to /webconsole/APIController. The APICommonServlet processes uploaded files, has ProcessExecutor capabilities, and handles XML/tar file operations. This is the most promising pre-auth RCE vector.
high_priority_attack_vectors.evidence: RequestCheckFilter.class line ~58-70: URI equals check for '/webconsole/APIController' bypasses multipart blocking. APICommonServlet.class strings: 'ProcessExecutor', 'exec', 'executeProcess', 'FileXMLinDir', 'FileXMLOutDir', 'createNewFile'
high_priority_attack_vectors.attack_class: File upload -> XML parsing -> command injection / path traversal

high_priority_attack_vectors.description: Pre-auth guest user creation via GuestUserHelper. Performs SQL queries and has ProcessExecutor access. Input validation quality unknown -- SQL injection or command injection possible if user-supplied data reaches SQL queries or shell commands without sanitization.
high_priority_attack_vectors.evidence: AuthConstants: mode 746, opcode 'add_guest_user', helper class 'cyberoam.identity.helpers.GuestUserHelper'. GuestUserHelper strings: 'sqlQuery', 'preparedQuery', 'ProcessExecutor', 'openorselective', multiple SQL SELECT statements with parameter markers (?)
high_priority_attack_vectors.attack_class: SQL injection, command injection, user enumeration

high_priority_attack_vectors.description: Admin login handler via WebAdminAuth. Performs SQL queries for credential validation. SQL injection in username/password fields would bypass authentication. Uses PreparedStatementBuffer (parameterized queries) but inline concatenation may exist.
high_priority_attack_vectors.attack_class: SQL injection in login, authentication bypass, timing attacks

high_priority_attack_vectors.description: Unknown pre-auth mode with no opcode. Needs class decompilation to determine functionality. Could be a maintenance/diagnostic endpoint.
Unknown pre-auth mode shared between webconsole and userportal. Present in both pre-auth operation maps. Needs class decompilation.

SSO admin controller exempt from session check. Handles SAML/SSO authentication flows. SSO implementations historically have XML parsing (XXE), signature validation bypasses, and assertion manipulation vulnerabilities.
high_priority_attack_vectors.attack_class: XXE, SAML assertion manipulation, authentication bypass

high_priority_attack_vectors.endpoint: Static resources and JSP pages accessible pre-auth
high_priority_attack_vectors.description: login.jsp, EULA.jsp, ApplianceNotRegistered.jsp, and all static resources (CSS, JS, images) under /webconsole/ are accessible without authentication. JavaScript files may reveal API endpoints, parameter names, hidden functionality, and client-side validation logic.

notable_observations: The 'cyberoam' package namespace confirms this codebase predates the Sophos acquisition -- significant legacy code
ProcessExecutor class is referenced in multiple pre-auth code paths (APICommonServlet, GuestUserHelper) -- shell command execution capability
SQL queries use a mix of PreparedStatementBuffer (safe) and direct string concatenation (potentially unsafe) -- need per-query analysis
ModSecurity WAF with CRS SQLi rules provides defense-in-depth but can be bypassed with encoding tricks
commons-fileupload-1.3.3 used for multipart handling -- check for CVE-2023-24998 (DoS) and CVE-2016-1000031 (RCE in older versions)
json-20090211.jar is extremely old (2009) -- check for known deserialization vulnerabilities
bcprov-jdk14-138.jar is ancient Bouncy Castle -- numerous known CVEs
Jetty 10.0.20 has known CVEs (check for CVE-2023-26048, CVE-2023-26049 cookie/multipart issues)
ApproveAliases is enabled in webconsole.xml -- allows symbolic link traversal in static resources
next_steps: CRITICAL: Decompile APICommonServlet fully -- the pre-auth multipart upload path with ProcessExecutor is the highest-priority target
Decompile GuestUserHelper to trace SQL query construction for injection
Decompile WebAdminAuth login flow for auth bypass / SQLi
Identify mode 6000, 8100, 4635 operation handlers via CyberoamCommonServlet dispatch logic
Decompile SSOAdminServlet for SAML/SSO attack vectors
Install cfr or procyon Java decompiler for cleaner analysis than javap bytecode
CVE check all library versions (json-20090211, bcprov-jdk14-138 are extremely old)
Analyze JavaScript files in webconsole for hidden API endpoints and parameter names
